Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8180005009978 == Commons category (P373) == * Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市大和町於保郷中2311', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|大神神社 (一宮市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 大神社 |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
社殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|大和町於保|Q126900259}}郷中2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|神八井耳命|Q11588756}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月20日|Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=大神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|鳥居]] '''大神社'''（おおじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|大和町於保|Q126900259}}（おほ）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の1柱。 * '''{{jalink|神八井耳命|Q11588756}}''' （かむやいみみのみこと） - {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}（初代）{{jalink|皇子|Q12886381}}で、{{jalink|多氏|Q11430816}}祖。 相殿に八王子を配祀するとする史料もあるが、これは当社が{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}に「天王社」と称していたことに由来するという{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。 === 歴史 === === 概史 === 創建は不詳。社名・地名に見えるように、当地に住んだ{{jalink|多氏|Q11430816}}（おおうじ）一族がその氏神として祖神の{{jalink|神八井耳命|Q11588756}}を祀ったといわれる{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。この多氏は{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|十市郡|Q11405036}}飫富郷（現・{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|磯城郡|Q1155036}}{{jalink|田原本町|Q1203159}}）付近を根拠とした古代氏族で、同地には氏神の{{jalink|多坐弥志理都比古神社|Q11430652}}（多神社とも。式内名神大社）が鎮座する。 {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、{{jalink|仁寿|Q1373077}}3年（{{jalink|853年|Q30367}}）に「多天神」が名神に列したと見えるほか、同年に多天神に従五位上の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}が授けられたと見え、また{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}元年（{{jalink|877年|Q23905}}）に「多名神」が正五位上に昇叙されたとある{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}なお『日本文徳天皇実録』嘉祥3年（850年）9月26日条には「尾張大神社」と見え、これを大神社に比定する説もあるが、一般には{{jalink|熱田神宮|Q482065}}に比定される {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。。 また{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|中島郡|Q780420}}に「太神社 名神大」と記載され、{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}に列している{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。写本によってその社名は「太神神社」「大神神社」とも記されるが、その場合の「神」の1字は衍字とされる{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。『延喜式』臨時祭 名神祭条においては、「太神社一座 或太作多」として「多神社」とも作る旨が記されている。古くから当地には地名として「於保（おほ）」が残ることに基づき、これらの記載の神社は当社にあたるとされる{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。なお『{{jalink|和名抄}}』に見える郷名のうちでは、当地は中島郡川埼郷に比定される{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。 その後の経緯は詳らかでなく、氏人が散亡したことにより荒廃し、{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}には「天王社」と称されていたという{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。現在、宮司は一宮市内の{{jalink|大神神社 (一宮市)|Q546197|大神神社}}ほか複数の神社の宮司を兼務する。 === 神階 === ; {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168}}時代における{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}奉叙の記録 * {{jalink|仁寿|Q1373077}}3年（{{jalink|853年|Q30367}}）{{jalink|6月11日|Q2617}} - 名神に列す （『{{jalink|日本文徳天皇実録|Q1052905}}』） - 表記は「多天神」。 * 仁寿3年（853年）{{jalink|7月12日|Q2688}} - 従五位上 （『日本文徳天皇実録』） - 表記は「多天神」。 * {{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}元年（{{jalink|877年|Q23905}}）閏{{jalink|2月26日|Q2359}} - 正五位下から正五位上 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「多名神」。 ; 六国史以後 * 従一位[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 『神社覈録 上編』]（皇典研究所、1902年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）413コマ。または正二位上{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 『国内神名帳』]}}（文政13年（1830年）転写合本、愛知県図書館「貴重和本デジタルライブラリー」より）6コマ。 （『{{jalink|尾張国内神名帳|Q11041830}}』） - 表記は「於保名神」。位階は写本により異同がある。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=大神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 23 愛知県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=田邊裕|chapter=太神神社|year=1989|title=式内社調査報告 第8巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === 外部リンク === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|太神社}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{Expand Japanese|大神社 (一宮市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Oo Shrine | native_name = 大神社 (一宮市) | image = Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Oo Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.271935|136.797892}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|lt=Kamuyaimimi-no-mikoto|en|Kamuyaimimi|fr|Kamuyaimimi|ja|神八井耳命|ko|카무야이미미노미코토|simple|Kamuyaimimi|zh|神八井耳命|WD=Q11588756}} }} {{nihongo|'''Oo Shrine'''|大神社 (一宮市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8180005009978 == Commons category (P373) == * Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市大和町於保郷中2311', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|大神神社 (一宮市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 大神社 |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
社殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|大和町於保|Q126900259}}郷中2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|神八井耳命|Q11588756}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月20日|Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=大神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|鳥居]] '''大神社'''（おおじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|大和町於保|Q126900259}}（おほ）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の1柱。 * '''{{jalink|神八井耳命|Q11588756}}''' （かむやいみみのみこと） - {{jalink|神武天皇|Q200188}}（初代）{{jalink|皇子|Q12886381}}で、{{jalink|多氏|Q11430816}}祖。 相殿に八王子を配祀するとする史料もあるが、これは当社が{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}に「天王社」と称していたことに由来するという{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。 === 歴史 === === 概史 === 創建は不詳。社名・地名に見えるように、当地に住んだ{{jalink|多氏|Q11430816}}（おおうじ）一族がその氏神として祖神の{{jalink|神八井耳命|Q11588756}}を祀ったといわれる{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。この多氏は{{jalink|大和国|Q907495}}{{jalink|十市郡|Q11405036}}飫富郷（現・{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|磯城郡|Q1155036}}{{jalink|田原本町|Q1203159}}）付近を根拠とした古代氏族で、同地には氏神の{{jalink|多坐弥志理都比古神社|Q11430652}}（多神社とも。式内名神大社）が鎮座する。 {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、{{jalink|仁寿|Q1373077}}3年（{{jalink|853年|Q30367}}）に「多天神」が名神に列したと見えるほか、同年に多天神に従五位上の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}が授けられたと見え、また{{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}元年（{{jalink|877年|Q23905}}）に「多名神」が正五位上に昇叙されたとある{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}なお『日本文徳天皇実録』嘉祥3年（850年）9月26日条には「尾張大神社」と見え、これを大神社に比定する説もあるが、一般には{{jalink|熱田神宮|Q482065}}に比定される {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。。 また{{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|中島郡|Q780420}}に「太神社 名神大」と記載され、{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}に列している{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。写本によってその社名は「太神神社」「大神神社」とも記されるが、その場合の「神」の1字は衍字とされる{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。『延喜式』臨時祭 名神祭条においては、「太神社一座 或太作多」として「多神社」とも作る旨が記されている。古くから当地には地名として「於保（おほ）」が残ることに基づき、これらの記載の神社は当社にあたるとされる{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。なお『{{jalink|和名抄}}』に見える郷名のうちでは、当地は中島郡川埼郷に比定される{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。 その後の経緯は詳らかでなく、氏人が散亡したことにより荒廃し、{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}には「天王社」と称されていたという{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}。現在、宮司は一宮市内の{{jalink|大神神社 (一宮市)|Q546197|大神神社}}ほか複数の神社の宮司を兼務する。 === 神階 === ; {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168}}時代における{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}奉叙の記録 * {{jalink|仁寿|Q1373077}}3年（{{jalink|853年|Q30367}}）{{jalink|6月11日|Q2617}} - 名神に列す （『{{jalink|日本文徳天皇実録|Q1052905}}』） - 表記は「多天神」。 * 仁寿3年（853年）{{jalink|7月12日|Q2688}} - 従五位上 （『日本文徳天皇実録』） - 表記は「多天神」。 * {{jalink|元慶|Q747451}}元年（{{jalink|877年|Q23905}}）閏{{jalink|2月26日|Q2359}} - 正五位下から正五位上 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「多名神」。 ; 六国史以後 * 従一位[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 『神社覈録 上編』]（皇典研究所、1902年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）413コマ。または正二位上{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 『国内神名帳』]}}（文政13年（1830年）転写合本、愛知県図書館「貴重和本デジタルライブラリー」より）6コマ。 （『{{jalink|尾張国内神名帳|Q11041830}}』） - 表記は「於保名神」。位階は写本により異同がある。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=大神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 23 愛知県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=田邊裕|chapter=太神神社|year=1989|title=式内社調査報告 第8巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === 外部リンク === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|太神社}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-tai}})
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}} (Oho), {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}), and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} is {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{jalink|pillar|Q4817}}: * '''{{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{jalink|prince|Q12886381}} of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}), where their tutelary {{jalink|Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official histories}}, in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{jalink|divine rank|Q11591025}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{jalink|Atsuta Jingū|Q482065}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Nakajima District|Q780420}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{jalink|Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|Ōgami Shrine}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168}} period * {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) {{jalink|June 11|Q2617}} - listed as myojin ('{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{jalink|July 12|Q2688}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}) intercalary {{jalink|February 26|Q2359}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{jalink|Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-tai}})
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}} (Oho), {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}), and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} is {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{jalink|pillar|Q4817}}: * '''{{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{jalink|prince|Q12886381}} of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}), where their tutelary {{jalink|Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official histories}}, in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{jalink|divine rank|Q11591025}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{jalink|Atsuta Jingū|Q482065}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Nakajima District|Q780420}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{jalink|Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|Ōgami Shrine}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168}} period * {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) {{jalink|June 11|Q2617}} - listed as myojin ('{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{jalink|July 12|Q2688}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}) intercalary {{jalink|February 26|Q2359}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{jalink|Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-tai}})
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}} (Oho), {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}), and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} is {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{jalink|pillar|Q4817}}: * '''{{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{jalink|prince|Q12886381}} of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}), where their tutelary {{jalink|Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official histories}}, in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{jalink|divine rank|Q11591025}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{jalink|Atsuta Jingū|Q482065}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Nakajima District|Q780420}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{jalink|Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|Ōgami Shrine}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168}} period * {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) {{jalink|June 11|Q2617}} - listed as myojin ('{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{jalink|July 12|Q2688}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}) intercalary {{jalink|February 26|Q2359}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{jalink|Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Shrine building |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-tai}})
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}} (Oho), {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}), and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} is {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{jalink|pillar|Q4817}}: * '''{{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{jalink|prince|Q12886381}} of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}), where their tutelary {{jalink|Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official histories}}, in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{jalink|divine rank|Q11591025}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{jalink|Atsuta Jingū|Q482065}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Nakajima District|Q780420}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{jalink|Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|Ōgami Shrine}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168}} period * {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) {{jalink|June 11|Q2617}} - listed as myojin ('{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{jalink|July 12|Q2688}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}) intercalary {{jalink|February 26|Q2359}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{jalink|Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-tai}})
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}} (Oho), {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}), and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} is {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{jalink|pillar|Q4817}}: * '''{{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{jalink|prince|Q12886381}} of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}), where their tutelary {{jalink|Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official histories}}, in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{jalink|divine rank|Q11591025}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{jalink|Atsuta Jingū|Q482065}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Nakajima District|Q780420}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{jalink|Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|Ōgami Shrine}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168}} period * {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) {{jalink|June 11|Q2617}} - listed as myojin ('{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{jalink|July 12|Q2688}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}) intercalary {{jalink|February 26|Q2359}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{jalink|Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]]

category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-tai}})
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}} (Oho), {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}), and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} is {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{jalink|pillar|Q4817}}: * '''{{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{jalink|prince|Q12886381}} of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}), where their tutelary {{jalink|Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official histories}}, in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{jalink|divine rank|Q11591025}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{jalink|Atsuta Jingū|Q482065}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Nakajima District|Q780420}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{jalink|Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|Ōgami Shrine}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168}} period * {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) {{jalink|June 11|Q2617}} - listed as myojin ('{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{jalink|July 12|Q2688}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}) intercalary {{jalink|February 26|Q2359}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{jalink|Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]]

category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-tai}})
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}} (Oho), {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}), and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} is {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{jalink|pillar|Q4817}}: * '''{{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{jalink|prince|Q12886381}} of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}), where their tutelary {{jalink|Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official histories}}, in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{jalink|divine rank|Q11591025}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{jalink|Atsuta Jingū|Q482065}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Nakajima District|Q780420}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{jalink|Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|Ōgami Shrine}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168}} period * {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) {{jalink|June 11|Q2617}} - listed as myojin ('{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{jalink|July 12|Q2688}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}) intercalary {{jalink|February 26|Q2359}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{jalink|Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-tai}})
former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 20|Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259}} (Oho), {{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}), and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|old ranking}} is {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{jalink|pillar|Q4817}}: * '''{{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{jalink|prince|Q12886381}} of {{jalink|Emperor Jimmu|Q200188}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{jalink|Ō clan|Q11430816}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{jalink|Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{jalink|Tōichi District|Q11405036}}, {{jalink|Yamato Province|Q907495}} (present {{jalink|Tawaramoto|Q1203159}}, {{jalink|Shiki District|Q1155036}}, {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}), where their tutelary {{jalink|Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168|official histories}}, in {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{jalink|divine rank|Q11591025}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{jalink|Atsuta Jingū|Q482065}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' {{jalink|Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{jalink|Enchō|Q1327274}} 5 ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}), under {{jalink|Nakajima District|Q780420}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{jalink|Myojin-taisha|Q9610964}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{jalink|Wamyōshō}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{jalink|early modern period|Q5308718}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{jalink|Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|Ōgami Shrine}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{jalink|Rikkokushi|Q1151168}} period * {{jalink|Ninju|Q1373077}} 3 ({{jalink|853|Q30367}}) {{jalink|June 11|Q2617}} - listed as myojin ('{{jalink|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{jalink|July 12|Q2688}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{jalink|Gangyō|Q747451}} 1 ({{jalink|877|Q23905}}) intercalary {{jalink|February 26|Q2359}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{jalink|Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-tai}})
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259}} (Oho), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking}} is {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{ill|lt=pillar|Q4817|WD=Q4817}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} (present {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}), where their tutelary {{ill|lt=Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652|WD=Q11430652}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official histories}}, in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingū|Q482065|WD=Q482065}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), under {{ill|lt=Nakajima District|Q780420|WD=Q780420}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{ill||lt=Wamyōshō|UNKNOWN}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{ill|lt=Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|WD=Q546197|lt=Ōgami Shrine}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168}} period * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367}}) {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617}} - listed as myojin ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{ill|lt=July 12|Q2688|WD=Q2688}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}) intercalary {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|WD=Q2359}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{ill|lt=Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-tai}})
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259}} (Oho), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking}} is {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{ill|lt=pillar|Q4817|WD=Q4817}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} (present {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}), where their tutelary {{ill|lt=Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652|WD=Q11430652}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official histories}}, in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingū|Q482065|WD=Q482065}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), under {{ill|lt=Nakajima District|Q780420|WD=Q780420}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|UNKNOWN}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{ill|lt=Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|WD=Q546197|lt=Ōgami Shrine}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168}} period * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367}}) {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617}} - listed as myojin ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{ill|lt=July 12|Q2688|WD=Q2688}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}) intercalary {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|WD=Q2359}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{ill|lt=Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]
