category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-tai}})
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259}} (Oho), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking}} is {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{ill|lt=pillar|Q4817|WD=Q4817}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} (present {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}), where their tutelary {{ill|lt=Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652|WD=Q11430652}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official histories}}, in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingū|Q482065|WD=Q482065}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), under {{ill|lt=Nakajima District|Q780420|WD=Q780420}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{ill|lt=Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|WD=Q546197|lt=Ōgami Shrine}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168}} period * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367}}) {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617}} - listed as myojin ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{ill|lt=July 12|Q2688|WD=Q2688}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}) intercalary {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|WD=Q2359}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{ill|lt=Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}{{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-tai}})
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259}} (Oho), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking}} is {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{ill|lt=pillar|Q4817|WD=Q4817}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} (present {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}), where their tutelary {{ill|lt=Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652|WD=Q11430652}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official histories}}, in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingū|Q482065|WD=Q482065}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), under {{ill|lt=Nakajima District|Q780420|WD=Q780420}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{ill|lt=Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|WD=Q546197|lt=Ōgami Shrine}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168}} period * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367}}) {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617}} - listed as myojin ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{ill|lt=July 12|Q2688|WD=Q2688}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}) intercalary {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|WD=Q2359}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{ill|lt=Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259}} (Oho), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking}} is {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{ill|lt=pillar|Q4817|WD=Q4817}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}} (present {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}), where their tutelary {{ill|lt=Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652|WD=Q11430652}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official histories}}, in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingū|Q482065|WD=Q482065}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}), under {{ill|lt=Nakajima District|Q780420|WD=Q780420}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{ill|lt=Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|WD=Q546197|lt=Ōgami Shrine}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168}} period * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367}}) {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617}} - listed as myojin ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{ill|lt=July 12|Q2688|WD=Q2688}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905}}) intercalary {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|WD=Q2359}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{ill|lt=Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259|1=Yamato-chō Oho}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259|1=Yamato-chō Oho}} (Oho), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{ill|lt=pillar|Q4817|WD=Q4817|1=Column}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381|1=Imperial Prince}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816|1=Ō clan}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816|1=Ō clan}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (present {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}), where their tutelary {{ill|lt=Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652|WD=Q11430652|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingū|Q482065|WD=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), under {{ill|lt=Nakajima District|Q780420|WD=Q780420|1=Nakashima district}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{ill|lt=Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|WD=Q546197|lt=Ōgami Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}} period * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}) {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617|1=June 11}} - listed as myojin ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{ill|lt=July 12|Q2688|WD=Q2688|1=July 12}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}) intercalary {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|WD=Q2359|1=February 26}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{ill|lt=Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8180005009978 == Commons category (P373) == * Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市大和町於保郷中2311', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259|1=Yamato-chō Oho}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259|1=Yamato-chō Oho}} (Oho), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{ill|lt=pillar|Q4817|WD=Q4817|1=Column}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381|1=Imperial Prince}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816|1=Ō clan}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816|1=Ō clan}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (present {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}), where their tutelary {{ill|lt=Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652|WD=Q11430652|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingū|Q482065|WD=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), under {{ill|lt=Nakajima District|Q780420|WD=Q780420|1=Nakashima district}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{ill|lt=Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|WD=Q546197|lt=Ōgami Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}} period * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}) {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617|1=June 11}} - listed as myojin ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{ill|lt=July 12|Q2688|WD=Q2688|1=July 12}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}) intercalary {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|WD=Q2359|1=February 26}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{ill|lt=Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8180005009978 == Commons category (P373) == * Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市大和町於保郷中2311', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259|1=Yamato-chō Oho}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259|1=Yamato-chō Oho}} (Oho), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{ill|lt=pillar|Q4817|WD=Q4817|1=Column}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381|1=Imperial Prince}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816|1=Ō clan}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816|1=Ō clan}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (present {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}), where their tutelary {{ill|lt=Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652|WD=Q11430652|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingū|Q482065|WD=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), under {{ill|lt=Nakajima District|Q780420|WD=Q780420|1=Nakashima district}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{ill|lt=Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|WD=Q546197|lt=Ōgami Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}} period * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}) {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617|1=June 11}} - listed as myojin ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{ill|lt=July 12|Q2688|WD=Q2688|1=July 12}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}) intercalary {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|WD=Q2359|1=February 26}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{ill|lt=Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259|1=Yamato-chō Oho}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259|1=Yamato-chō Oho}} (Oho), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{ill|lt=pillar|Q4817|WD=Q4817|1=Column}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381|1=Imperial Prince}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816|1=Ō clan}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816|1=Ō clan}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (present {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}), where their tutelary {{ill|lt=Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652|WD=Q11430652|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingū|Q482065|WD=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), under {{ill|lt=Nakajima District|Q780420|WD=Q780420|1=Nakashima district}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{ill|lt=Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|WD=Q546197|lt=Ōgami Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}} period * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}) {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617|1=June 11}} - listed as myojin ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{ill|lt=July 12|Q2688|WD=Q2688|1=July 12}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}) intercalary {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|WD=Q2359|1=February 26}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{ill|lt=Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおしんしや いちのみや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259|1=Yamato-chō Oho}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259|1=Yamato-chō Oho}} (Oho), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{ill|lt=pillar|Q4817|WD=Q4817|1=Column}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381|1=Imperial Prince}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816|1=Ō clan}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816|1=Ō clan}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (present {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}), where their tutelary {{ill|lt=Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652|WD=Q11430652|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingū|Q482065|WD=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), under {{ill|lt=Nakajima District|Q780420|WD=Q780420|1=Nakashima district}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{ill|lt=Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|WD=Q546197|lt=Ōgami Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}} period * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}) {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617|1=June 11}} - listed as myojin ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{ill|lt=July 12|Q2688|WD=Q2688|1=July 12}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}) intercalary {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|WD=Q2359|1=February 26}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{ill|lt=Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8180005009978 == Commons category (P373) == * Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市大和町於保郷中2311', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} [[JA:大神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438337}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Ōmiwa Shrine (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Oo Shrine |画像 = [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine building |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259|1=Yamato-chō Oho}}Gōnaka 2311 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-tai|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Oo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Oo Shrine''' (おおじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato-chō Oho|Q126900259|WD=Q126900259|1=Yamato-chō Oho}} (Oho), {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=old ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines one {{ill|lt=pillar|Q4817|WD=Q4817|1=Column}}: * '''{{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}}''' (かむやいみみのみこと) - a {{ill|lt=prince|Q12886381|WD=Q12886381|1=Imperial Prince}} of {{ill|lt=Emperor Jimmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} (1st emperor), ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816|1=Ō clan}}. Some sources say Hachiōji is enshrined in the aiden, which is said to derive from this shrine being called "Tennō-sha" in the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. === History === === Overview === Founding is unknown. As seen in both shrine and place names, it is said the {{ill|lt=Ō clan|Q11430816|WD=Q11430816|1=Ō clan}} (Ōuji), who lived here, enshrined their ancestral deity {{ill|lt=Kamuyai-mimi-no-Mikoto|Q11588756|WD=Q11588756|1=Kamuyaimimi}} as clan tutelary deity{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. This clan was an ancient lineage based around Oho-gō in {{ill|lt=Tōichi District|Q11405036|WD=Q11405036|1=Toichi district}}, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} (present {{ill|lt=Tawaramoto|Q1203159|WD=Q1203159|1=Tawaramoto, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Shiki District|Q1155036|WD=Q1155036|1=Shiki District, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}), where their tutelary {{ill|lt=Ō ni Imasu Mishiritsuhiko Shrine|Q11430652|WD=Q11430652|1=Oonimasumishiritsuhiko Shrine}} (also called Ō Shrine; a Shikinaisha Myojin-taisha) is located. In the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=official histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}) "Ō tenjin" is listed as a myojin, and in the same year was given {{ill|lt=divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} Junior Fifth Upper; then in {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}), "Ō myojin" was promoted to Senior Fifth Upper{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}In '{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}},' Kaśhō 3 (850), 9th month, day 26, "Owari Ō Shrine" appears; a theory identifies this with Oo Shrine, but it is generally identified with {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingū|Q482065|WD=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} {{Harv|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}.. Also, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}), under {{ill|lt=Nakajima District|Q780420|WD=Q780420|1=Nakashima district}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, it is listed as "Ō Shrine, Myojin-tai," and ranked as {{ill|lt=Myojin-taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In manuscript copies the name is written both "Ōgami Shrine" and "Ōkamisha," but in that case one "神" character is treated as an interpolation{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the Engishiki temporary-festival myojin-festival section it also says: "Ō Shrine one seat, alternatively written Ta-sakuta," indicating a form "Ta Shrine." Because the place name "Oho" has long remained in this area, these entries are taken to refer to this shrine{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Wamyōshō|1=UNKNOWN}},' this area is identified with Kawasaki-gō, Nakajima District{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Later details are unclear. It is said to have declined as clan members scattered, and by the {{ill|lt=early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718|1=Early modern period}} it was called "Tennō-sha"{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. After the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}. Today, its chief priest concurrently serves several shrines, including {{ill|lt=Ōgami Shrine (Ichinomiya)|Q546197|WD=Q546197|lt=Ōgami Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya}} in Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === ; Records of bestowal in the {{ill|lt=Rikkokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|1=Rikkokushi}} period * {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|WD=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 3 ({{ill|lt=853|Q30367|WD=Q30367|1=853}}) {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|WD=Q2617|1=June 11}} - listed as myojin ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}') - written as "Ō tenjin". * Ninju 3 (853) {{ill|lt=July 12|Q2688|WD=Q2688|1=July 12}} - Junior Fifth Upper ('Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku') - written as "Ō tenjin". * {{ill|lt=Gangyō|Q747451|WD=Q747451|1=Gangyō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=877|Q23905|WD=Q23905|1=877}}) intercalary {{ill|lt=February 26|Q2359|WD=Q2359|1=February 26}} - promoted from Senior Fifth Lower to Senior Fifth Upper ('{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}') - written as "Ō myojin". ; After the Rikkokushi period * Junior First[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 'Jinja Kakuroku, upper volume'] (Kōten Kenkyūjo, 1902, NDL Digital Collection), frame 413. or Senior Second Upper{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 'Kokunai Jinmyōchō']}} (copied/combined in Bunsei 13 (1830), Aichi Prefectural Library "Digital Library of Rare Japanese Books"), frame 6. ('{{ill|lt=Owari Kuninai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|WD=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}}') - written as "Oho Myōjin." Rank differs by manuscript. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ō Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 23: Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai ed.|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ta Shrine|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|太神神社（式内社）|1989年}}}} === External links === * {{神道・神社史料集成|090401|Ta Shrine}} * {{Commonscat-inline|Oo-jinja (Ichinomiya)}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oo Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oo Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧村社]] [[Category:多氏|社おおしんしや いちのみや]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]
